page: Microsoft Azure FX48-24ms_v2 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the FXmsv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 24 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 12 physical Intel Xeon 8573C cores, operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and integer operations. This server is configured with 1008.0 GB of memory, yielding 42.0 GB of RAM per physical core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2356.0 and a text processing score of 2987.0, both placing in the top 10% of tested servers. Memory bandwidth is also strong, with read-only large block speeds reaching 187.78 GB/sec and write speeds at 120.35 GB/sec, placing in the top 25%. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also register in the top 25% tier. Multi-core performance, such as the Geekbench multi-core score of 16629.0 and the stress-ng multi-core score of 26664.47, falls within the average range.

The server lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, which represents a tradeoff for workloads requiring high-speed local scratch disks or hardware-accelerated graphics processing. However, its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and high-frequency dedicated CPU cores provide high resource density for memory-intensive applications. It is suited for large-scale database operations, memory-bound enterprise applications, and single-threaded processing tasks that benefit from high clock speeds and large cache sizes.
description: The Microsoft Azure FX48-24ms_v2 is a memory-optimized server from the FXmsv2 family, powered by a 12-core Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 24 dedicated vCPUs running at 4.2 GHz. It features 1008.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 42.0 GB per core, but includes no local storage or GPU hardware. Benchmark results show top-tier single-core performance, with Geekbench single-core and text processing scores in the top 10% of tested systems. Large-block memory bandwidth and single-threaded compression speeds also rank in the top 25%. Conversely, multi-core benchmarks and database operations align with average tiers. This configuration is suited for memory-intensive enterprise applications, large in-memory databases, and workloads requiring high clock speeds with substantial memory capacity.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX48-24ms_v2 is a memory-optimized server featuring 24 dedicated vCPUs, a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor, and 1008.0 GB of RAM, delivering top-tier single-core and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure FX48-24ms_v2 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server with 24 dedicated vCPUs, 1008.0 GB of RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU.
tagline: A memory-intensive server combining high-frequency dedicated Intel Xeon processing with over one terabyte of RAM for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 24 dedicated vCPUs powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8573C processor
1008.0 GB of RAM providing 42.0 GB of memory per physical core
Top-tier single-core performance and text processing benchmarks
Strong large-block memory read and write bandwidth
No local storage or GPU hardware included
Suited for in-memory databases and memory-bound enterprise applications
